attacking_style: Attacks on steepest gradients of decisive climbs, targeting vulnerable GC rivals. Capable of holding slim advantages into final meters through sustained power rather than explosive accelerations.
team_tactics: Sky provides measured support but Henao often operates with limited domestique support in finale. Relies more on individual strength than traditional Sky train tactics, possibly as opportunistic GC option.
terrain_preference: Steep gradient climbs on Cat 1 ascents where sustained power becomes decisive. Excels on penultimate and decisive mountain stages where cumulative fatigue favors diesel engines over punchy climbers.
vulnerability: Isolation tactics can reduce his margin for error—succeeded despite only two functional domestiques but suggests dependence on maintaining position rather than recovering it. Time bonuses critical to narrow victories.
key_patterns: Targets steepest gradient sections to exploit rivals' weakness, documented across multiple stages.
Holds narrow time gaps through sustained effort rather than creating large advantages (2-30 seconds).
Emerges as GC threat on mountain stages, positioned as Sky's depth option beyond designated leaders.
Survives aggressive racing and chaos well, maintaining GC position through opening stages and finale pressure.
form_trajectory: positive: Progressively strengthened through Paris-Nice week, from surviving chaos to taking yellow to defending against Contador's attack
